title: Add Product to Initial Quantity
description: After finding a product representing additional items, add it to an existing starting amount.
condition: An initial quantity and an additional amount computed as (a × b) are given in a word problem.
response: Add the initial quantity and the computed product.
label: Principle
example: Initial 253 balloons and 6 packs of 30: \(6 \times 30 = 180\), then \(253 + 180 = 433\).

title: Add Two Monetary Amounts
description: Combine two given monetary amounts to find a total.
condition: Two monetary amounts are given and the total amount is needed.
response: Add the two amounts to find the total.
label: Principle / Model

title: Apply Associative Property of Multiplication
description: Regroup factors in a multiplication expression without changing the product.
condition: A multiplication expression of the form a × (b × c).
response: Rewrite as (a × b) × c.
label: Principle

title: Apply Distributive Property
description: Use the distributive property to break apart a factor into easier parts and multiply each part before adding.
condition: Expression of the form (a + b) × c or decomposing a factor such as 14 × 3 into (10 + 4) × 3
response: Compute (a × c) + (b × c)
label: Principle / Model

title: Apply Given Division Pattern
description: Use a stated rule to divide each input value by a specified number.
condition: A pattern states to divide each input by a fixed number (e.g., 7).
response: Divide each input value by the given number to produce the output.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Basic Division Facts
description: Recall or compute the quotient of two whole numbers within basic multiplication facts.
condition: Division expression a ÷ b with whole numbers within multiplication table range.
response: State the correct quotient.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Often relies on memorized multiplication facts. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Compute Basic Multiplication Facts
description: Recall or compute the product of two whole numbers within basic multiplication facts.
condition: Multiplication expression a × b with whole numbers within multiplication table range.
response: State the correct product.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Fluency with multiplication facts. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Decompose Dividend Using Partial Quotients
description: Break apart a dividend into addends that are easier to divide, then divide each part and add the quotients.
condition: Division expression a ÷ b where a can be decomposed into convenient addends (e.g., 72 = 40 + 32).
response: Rewrite as (a1 ÷ b) + (a2 ÷ b) and compute partial quotients, then add them.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Uses distributive property of division over addition.

title: Decompose Multiple of Ten
description: Rewrite a multiple of ten as a single-digit number times 10.
condition: A number ending in zero (e.g., 50, 40).
response: Express the number as (digit × 10).
label: Principle

title: Divide Total by Unit Cost
description: Find the number of equal items that can be purchased by dividing total money by the cost per item.
condition: A total amount of money and a cost per item are given; the number of items is unknown.
response: Divide the total amount by the unit cost.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the total is $63 and each bracelet costs $9, compute $63 \div 9 = 7$ bracelets.

title: Evaluate Basic Multiplication and Division Facts
description: Compute products and quotients involving 0 and 1 using basic arithmetic properties.
condition: An equation involving multiplication or division by 0 or 1 (e.g., $0 \times 1$, $1 \times 9$, $0 \div 1$).
response: Compute the product or quotient using identity and zero properties.
label: Principle / Model
example: $1 \times 9 = 9$ because multiplying by 1 leaves the number unchanged; $0 \div 1 = 0$.

title: Evaluate Expression for Equality
description: Determine whether two expressions have the same value by evaluating them.
condition: Given two expressions and asked if they are equal
response: Compute both expressions and state Yes if equal, No if not
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate Expressions with Parentheses
description: Evaluate numerical expressions by first computing operations inside parentheses, then completing remaining operations.
condition: Expression containing parentheses with addition and/or multiplication.
response: Compute inside parentheses first, then complete remaining operations to find the value.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Order of operations at elementary level (parentheses first).

title: Identify and Apply Multiplicative Pattern
description: Determine that outputs are produced by multiplying inputs by a constant factor and apply that factor.
condition: A table shows consistent input-output pairs where each output equals input multiplied by the same number.
response: State the multiplication rule and multiply new inputs by that constant.
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Division as Missing Factor
description: Represent a division equation as a related multiplication equation with a missing factor.
condition: Equation of the form a ÷ b = n
response: Rewrite as n × b = a (or b × n = a)
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Equal Groups in Word Problem
description: Translate equal groups described in words into a multiplication expression.
condition: A context describing equal groups (e.g., sheets with rows, packs with items).
response: Write a multiplication expression representing groups × items per group (and multiply sequential factors if needed).
label: Principle
example: 7 sheets with 5 rows of 6 stickers each → \(7 \times 5 \times 6\).

title: Match Equation to Correct Value
description: Determine which given number makes an equation true.
condition: Equation with one unknown and a list of possible values
response: Select the value that satisfies the equation
label: Production / Skill

title: Multiply by a Multiple of Ten
description: Multiply a whole number by a multiple of ten by multiplying non-zero digits and appending one zero.
condition: An expression of the form a × (b0), where b0 is a multiple of 10.
response: Compute (a × b) and append one zero to the product.
label: Principle

title: Represent Unknown with Variable
description: Use a letter to represent an unknown quantity in an equation.
condition: Word problem specifies an unknown quantity and instructs to use a specific letter.
response: Write an equation using the given letter to represent the unknown.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Let $b$ be books per shelf: $28 \div 7 = b$.

title: Solve for Missing Factor
description: Find the unknown number in a multiplication or division equation.
condition: Equation of the form a × x = c or c ÷ a = x
response: Compute the missing number that makes the equation true
label: Rule / Plan
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Solve Two-Step Multiplicative Word Problem
description: Solve a word problem requiring multiplication to find a total and addition or subtraction to compare quantities.
condition: Context requires finding a product and then adjusting by adding or subtracting a given difference.
response: Compute product, then add or subtract given amount to find unknown quantity.
label: Principle / Model
example: Jayla has $5\times7=35$ rocks. If she has 15 more than David, then David has $35-15=20$.
notes: Requires identifying sequence of operations from context. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Translate Equal Groups to Division Equation
description: Represent a total split equally into groups using a division equation.
condition: Word problem describing total items shared equally among a given number of groups
response: Write equation total ÷ number_of_groups = amount_per_group
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Requires identifying total and number of groups.

title: Translate Equal Groups to Multiplication Equation
description: Represent equal groups with a multiplication equation.
condition: Word problem describing number of groups and number in each group
response: Write equation groups × number_in_each_group = total
label: Rule / Plan

title: Use Inverse Relationship of Multiplication and Division
description: Use the inverse relationship between multiplication and division to write related equations or find missing values.
condition: Division equation a ÷ b = c or missing factor equation b × c = a
response: Rewrite as the related multiplication or division equation
label: Principle / Model
